title: Iran Rejects Ceasefire Before April 6 Deadline; Trump Threatens 'Power Plant Day and Bridge Day'; War Enters Acute Escalation Phase
detail: Iran rejected the proposed 45-day ceasefire framework before Trump's hard deadline of Tuesday April 6 at 8:00 p.m. ET expired. Iranian officials refused to reopen the Strait of Hormuz as a precondition to any deal, maintaining that lifting sanctions must come first. Iran's Supreme National Security Council issued a statement calling Trump's ultimatum 'coercion at gunpoint' and asserting it would not 'reward aggression with unilateral concessions on sovereign territory.' In response, Trump posted to Truth Social: 'Tuesday will be Power Plant Day, and Bridge Day' and threatened to target Iranian energy infrastructure, power plants, and bridges. A follow-up post stated Iran should 'Open the F***in' Strait' or face the 'hardest strikes you have seen yet.' US military officials confirmed strike packages against Iranian power generation facilities and bridge infrastructure were pre-positioned and ready for execution. The CNBC live blog confirmed the April 6 deadline expired without agreement. The acute escalation represented a new phase in Operation Epic Fury, which entered its 36th day. Oil prices, which had retreated below $100/barrel on Easter ceasefire optimism, surged back toward $110 as the deadline expired. Trump's approval rating remained below 40% in the latest RealClearPolling average. Markets — reopening on Easter Monday after the Easter break and pricing in both the strong March NFP (+178,000 jobs) and the Iran deadline crisis — faced conflicting signals.

media.caption: Trump declares 'Power Plant Day, Bridge Day' as Iran rejects ceasefire before April 6 Hormuz deadline expires

title: Easter Monday: Markets Reopen Pricing In Strong March NFP (+178K) and Iran Deadline Crisis Simultaneously
detail: US equity markets reopened on Easter Monday, April 6, facing the unusual task of simultaneously pricing in two major competing signals: the strong March 2026 nonfarm payrolls report released on Good Friday (+178,000 jobs, unemployment 4.3%, beating estimates by approximately 120,000 jobs) and the expiry of Trump's Iran Strait of Hormuz deadline without agreement — followed by Trump's threat of 'Power Plant Day and Bridge Day' strikes. The strong NFP supported risk assets and reduced immediate recession fears, while the Iran crisis — with oil surging back toward $110/barrel on Hormuz closure concerns — weighed on sentiment. The S&P 500 had fallen approximately 7% year-to-date through Easter week. Health care stocks faced additional pressure from the April 2 pharmaceutical import tariff executive order (100% tariff on patented drugs). Recession probability models remained elevated: Moody's AI recession model at 49%; Goldman Sachs at 30% over 12 months. The FOMC March 2026 projections foresaw 4.4% unemployment by year-end; the March actual figure of 4.3% offered a modest upside surprise. Market analysts noted the unusual situation of a positive labor market surprise being overshadowed by geopolitical escalation risk from the Iran war.

media.caption: Wall Street prices in two conflicting signals on Easter Monday: blowout jobs report and Iran deadline escalation
